query: Status of Russia State Duma bill No. 1194918-8 'On Digital Currency and Digital Rights' second and third reading July 2026. Has the second reading occurred? When is third reading scheduled? Any news from July 2026 about the schedule.
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the summary:

## Status of Bill No. 1194918-8 "On Digital Currency and Digital Rights" — as of July 13, 2026

**Has the second reading occurred?** No. As of today, the second reading has **not yet taken place** in the State Duma plenary. What has happened is that the **Committee on Financial Markets approved the bill for its second reading** in early July 2026 (announced by committee chairman Anatoly Aksakov via Telegram around July 3–8).

**When is the third reading scheduled?** Aksakov stated the **second and third readings were tentatively scheduled for July 21, 2026** — both on the same sitting. That date is still upcoming relative to today (July 13), so neither reading has occurred yet.

**July 2026 news on the schedule:**
- **~July 3–8:** Financial Markets Committee endorsed the revised/final version for the second reading. Key changes: dropped the wallet-address disclosure requirement, allowed crypto-to-crypto swaps and broader payment use, whitelisted more cryptocurrencies, and simplified holder reporting.
- **July 21, 2026:** Tentative plenary date for both remaining readings.
- After Duma passage: still needs Federation Council approval + Putin's signature.
- **Targeted entry into force: September 1, 2026** (the original July 1 deadline was missed due to inter-agency delays acknowledged by the Finance Ministry in June).

**Important caveat:** The July 21 date was explicitly described as "tentative," and no source dated on/after July 21 confirming the readings actually happened was found — consistent with today's date preceding the scheduled reading. The bill has strong government backing (first reading passed 327–340 in April), so smooth advancement is widely expected, but the timeline has already slipped once.

sources.title: Russia to enforce new crypto law on September 1
sources.snippet: Second and third readings tentatively scheduled for July 21, per Anatoly Aksakov; law to enter into force September 1, 2026. Article dated July 3, 2026.

sources.title: Russia Advances Crypto Oversight Bill With Easier Reporting Rules
sources.snippet: Financial Markets Committee approved the revised bill for its main (second) parliamentary reading; drops wallet-address disclosure. Dated July 8, 2026.

sources.title: Russia's amended crypto bill to permit wider use of coins in payments
sources.snippet: Committee on Financial Markets approved the 'On Digital Currency and Digital Rights' bill for second reading; September deadline for adoption; crypto-to-crypto swaps allowed.

sources.title: Russia advances crypto bill after dropping wallet address disclosure rule
sources.snippet: State Duma committee approved revised bill for second reading, removing wallet-address disclosure, adding crypto-funded investment and transfer controls; per Aksakov's Telegram.

sources.title: Russia Advances Sweeping Crypto Regulation Bill
sources.snippet: First reading passed with 327 of 340 deputies in favor; bill titled 'On Digital Currency and Digital Rights,' introduced by the government.

sources.title: Russia's State Duma advances bill regulating crypto market, phasing out P2P transactions
sources.snippet: Bill submitted by government on April 1; passed first reading with 327 of 340 votes; introduces five types of regulated organizations.

sources.title: Russia expects delay in crypto market regulation bill
sources.snippet: Ministry of Finance expects a short delay in adoption; final adoption requires further readings and presidential approval, rollout later in the year.

sources.title: Russia delays digital currency law, prepares to ban crypto ads
sources.snippet: Legislation was expected to enter into force by July 1 at the latest; adoption delayed; government preparing crypto advertising ban.

data_points.metric: Tentatively scheduled date for second and third readings
